_note: Run python generate_fallbacks.py to populate this file with facts.
facts: The shortest war in history lasted only 38 to 45 minutes — the Anglo-Zanzibar War of 1896.
A group of flamingos is called a flamboyance.
Humans share 60% of their DNA with bananas.
The Eiffel Tower can be 15 cm taller during summer due to thermal expansion.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the construction of the Great Pyramid.
science: Water can boil and freeze at the same time — a phenomenon called the triple point.
A teaspoon of neutron star material would weigh about 10 million tons.
The human eye can detect a single photon of light in complete darkness.
DNA is so tightly packed that if all human DNA were stretched out, it would reach Pluto and back — 17 times.
Glass is technically a supercooled liquid, not a solid.
biology: Your stomach gets a new lining every 3–4 days to protect itself from its own acid.
The human liver performs over 500 different functions.
Bone is five times stronger than steel by weight.
Your nose can detect over 1 trillion different scents.
The human body has more bacterial cells than human cells — by a factor of 1.3 to 1.
history: Napoleon Bonaparte was actually of average height for his era — around 5'7".
Oxford University is older than the Aztec Empire.
The fax machine was invented before the telephone.
Ancient Romans used crushed mouse brains as toothpaste.
The first computer programmer was Ada Lovelace, who worked in the 1840s.
technology: The first iPhone had less processing power than a modern microwave.
Email was invented before the World Wide Web.
The first computer mouse was made of wood.
Google processes over 8.5 billion searches per day.
The average person spends over 6 hours per day on screens.
space: There are more stars in the universe than grains of sand on all of Earth's beaches.
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus.
Footprints left on the Moon will remain for at least 100 million years.
The Sun accounts for 99.86% of all mass in the Solar System.
There is a planet made mostly of diamond — 55 Cancri e.
psychology: The brain can process images in as little as 13 milliseconds.
Memories are reconstructed every time you recall them — meaning they change slightly each time.
People are more creative after a shower because warm water relaxes the prefrontal cortex.
The mere act of smiling can actually make you feel happier.
Multitasking reduces productivity by up to 40%.
health: Sleeping fewer than 7 hours a night doubles your risk of catching a cold.
The gut microbiome contains 100 trillion bacteria that influence mood and health.
A 20-minute nap improves alertness and performance more than caffeine.
Chronic loneliness is as damaging to your health as smoking 15 cigarettes a day.
Cold exposure activates brown fat cells, which burn energy to generate heat.
nature: Trees communicate and share resources through underground fungal networks called the Wood Wide Web.
A single mature oak tree can support over 500 species of insects, birds, and fungi.
Whale songs can travel thousands of miles through the ocean.
The largest living organism on Earth is a honey fungus in Oregon covering 8.9 km˛.
Lightning strikes Earth about 100 times every second.
animals: Crows can recognise and remember human faces for years.
Octopuses have three hearts, nine brains, and blue blood.
Tardigrades can survive in the vacuum of space, extreme radiation, and temperatures near absolute zero.
Elephants are the only animals known to hold funerals for their dead.
Pistol shrimp snap their claws so fast they create a flash of light hotter than the surface of the Sun.
food: Honey never spoils — archaeologists have found edible honey in 3,000-year-old Egyptian tombs.
Chocolate was once used as currency by the Aztecs.
Apples, pears, and roses all belong to the same plant family — Rosaceae.
The spiciness of chillies is measured in Scoville Heat Units.
Avocados are technically berries, while strawberries are not.
mathematics: Zero is the only number that cannot be represented by Roman numerals.
A googol is 1 followed by 100 zeros — and a googolplex has a googol of zeros.
The Fibonacci sequence appears in sunflower seeds, nautilus shells, and galaxy spirals.
Pi has been calculated to over 100 trillion decimal places — and no pattern has ever been found.
In a room of just 23 people, there is a 50% chance two share the same birthday.
geography: Canada has more lakes than all other countries combined.
Russia spans 11 time zones and covers 11% of Earth's land area.
The Sahara Desert is roughly the same size as the entire United States.
New Zealand was the first country to give women the right to vote, in 1893.
The Dead Sea is so salty that you float effortlessly — and it is 430m below sea level.
mythology: The word 'volcano' comes from Vulcan, the Roman god of fire.
In Norse mythology, the world will end in Ragnarok — a battle of gods and giants.
Ancient Greeks believed the heart was the seat of intelligence, not the brain.
The Egyptian god Thoth was said to have invented writing, mathematics, and science.
In Hindu mythology, the universe goes through endless cycles of creation and destruction.
inventions: The microwave was invented accidentally when radar waves melted a chocolate bar in an engineer's pocket.
Post-it notes were invented from a failed attempt to create a super-strong adhesive.
The World Wide Web was invented in 1989 by Tim Berners-Lee as an internal document system at CERN.
Velcro was inspired by burr seeds sticking to a dog's fur during a walk.
The first ATM was installed in London in 1967.
